title: CortexelErrorV1
description:
A single diagnostic. Diagnostics are themselves part of the untrusted-input boundary: a small validation failure must not be able to amplify a hostile payload through logs, agent context, repair prompts, and the DOM.

Two consequences follow. First, `actualSummary` is a bounded CATEGORY, never raw caller content. Second, a `repair` is structured DATA — never instruction-shaped prose that a downstream language model might read as a command.

code.description: Stable, append-only, and the thing tests assert on. Prose may improve; a code's meaning may not.

instancePath.description: An RFC 6901 JSON Pointer into the request. The empty string is the root. `~` and `/` are escaped identically in TypeScript and Python.

limit.description: For a resource failure: what the bound was and what was observed. Reporting the observed COUNT is safe; echoing the offending token is not.

actualSummary.description: A bounded category such as `string(length=1024)` or `<object>`. Never the value itself: rendering an untrusted value into a diagnostic would execute its toString/valueOf/Symbol.toPrimitive hook while handling an error.

repair.description: A machine-applicable correction. `applySafeRepairs` accepts only allow-listed operations and always revalidates from the start; `validate` never auto-applies one.

omittedCount.description: Only on ERROR_LIMIT_REACHED: how many diagnostics the budget suppressed. No failure is ever hidden without saying so.
